page: UpCloud STARTER-2xCPU-16GB is a virtual server instance configured with 2 vCPUs and 16.0 GB of RAM, running on the KVM hypervisor. The underlying hardware features an AMD EPYC 9575F processor utilizing the x86_64 architecture. The CPU allocation is shared, with 2 physical cores operating at a base speed of 2.0 GHz and supporting 1 thread per core. Cache specifications include 64 KB L1d cache, 0.5 MB L2 cache, and 16 MB L3 cache. The processor supports advanced instruction sets, including AVX, AVX512F, AVX512_VNNI, AVX_VNNI, AVX512_BF16, AES, VAES, and SHA_NI.

Storage is bundled as a 50 GB local SSD, and the instance includes one complimentary public IPv4 address. In terms of performance, the server demonstrates top-tier single-core CPU capabilities in stress-ng benchmarks and high single-threaded compression and decompression speeds. However, multi-threaded performance and overall PassMark CPU scores fall into the lower tiers, reflecting the shared CPU allocation model. Memory bandwidth is average, reaching 395.81 GB/sec for small cached blocks and dropping to 68.93 GB/sec for larger uncached blocks.

This configuration offers high memory density relative to its vCPU count, making it a qualitatively cost-effective option for memory-bound workloads that do not require sustained, dedicated multi-core compute power. It is suitable for lightweight databases, static web serving, caching layers, and basic text or small-scale language model inference.
description: UpCloud STARTER-2xCPU-16GB is an x86_64 virtual server featuring 2 shared vCPUs and 16.0 GB of RAM on a KVM hypervisor. Powered by an AMD EPYC 9575F processor running at 2.0 GHz, it includes a 50 GB local SSD and one public IPv4 address. Benchmark data reveals top-tier single-core CPU performance and single-threaded compression speeds, though multi-threaded operations and database benchmarks are weaker due to the shared CPU allocation. With an 8.0 GB RAM-per-core ratio, this instance provides a memory-optimized profile suitable for lightweight web serving, caching, and memory-intensive utility workloads.
og_description: UpCloud STARTER-2xCPU-16GB is a KVM-based virtual server featuring 2 shared AMD EPYC vCPUs, 16.0 GB of RAM, and a 50 GB local SSD, optimized for memory-intensive utility workloads.
meta_description: UpCloud STARTER-2xCPU-16GB is a KVM virtual server with 2 shared AMD EPYC vCPUs, 16.0 GB of RAM, and 50 GB of local SSD storage.
tagline: A shared-core virtual server combining high memory density with strong single-threaded performance for utility and caching workloads.
bullet_points: 2 shared AMD EPYC vCPUs and 16.0 GB of system memory
Bundled 50 GB local SSD storage and 1 public IPv4 address
Top-tier single-core CPU and single-threaded compression performance
Weaker multi-threaded and database performance due to shared CPU allocation
Best suited for caching, static web serving, and memory-heavy utility tasks
